name: Choosing sliding glass doors for foggy but mild coastal temperatures

mainEntity.name: In the mild but foggy climate of Pacifica and Daly City, how should I balance energy efficiency and condensation control when choosing a new sliding glass door?
mainEntity.text: In the mild but foggy climate of Pacifica and Daly City, how should I balance energy efficiency and condensation control when choosing a new sliding glass door?

acceptedAnswer.text: Mild coastal climates like Pacifica, Daly City, and Half Moon Bay rarely see extreme heat or cold, but frequent fog and high humidity make condensation and drafts real concerns. When selecting a sliding glass door, start by looking at the whole-unit U‑factor and air leakage rating. A lower U‑factor improves insulation, which helps keep interior glass surfaces warmer on cool, damp mornings, reducing visible condensation. Choose insulated glass with a low‑E coating appropriate for your region; you typically want a balance that limits heat loss without excessively blocking winter sun. Warm-edge spacers between panes and gas fills (such as argon) further help keep the inner pane temperature closer to room temperature. Equally important is the door frame: insulated fiberglass or high-quality vinyl frames reduce cold transfer better than bare aluminum. Pay close attention to air sealing and installation quality. Even a great door will feel drafty if the frame is not shimmed and sealed correctly. Ask for low air leakage ratings and confirm that your installer will use proper foam, sealants, and flashing. Inside the home, keeping humidity in check with ventilation fans and occasional window venting will further reduce condensation on cool, foggy days. Together, these choices yield a comfortable, efficient door without overpaying for specs designed for extreme climates.
acceptedAnswer.description: In foggy but mild coastal areas, prioritize sliding glass doors with low U‑factor and warm-edge spacers to reduce condensation, plus low‑E glass tuned for your climate. Because temperatures are moderate, focus on air sealing, quality weatherstripping, and insulated frames as much as extreme solar gain numbers. Good ventilation and interior humidity control further reduce foggy‑day condensation.
